page: Amazon Web Services i7i.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 2.4 GHz. The CPU architecture includes a 320 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

For local storage, the i7i.12xlarge includes a bundled 11,250 GB NVMe SSD configuration, making it highly suitable for data-intensive workloads. The network architecture delivers a baseline bandwidth of 28 Gbps. The combination of high-capacity local NVMe storage and fast DDR5 memory establishes a strong resource density for applications requiring rapid local data access without relying on external network-attached storage.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance across memory and processing tasks. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 2,319.0 and a multi-core score of 20,704.0, both placing in the top tier. Memory bandwidth tests showed strong results, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 4,099.27 GB/sec and large block uncached reads at 240.93 GB/sec. Database operations performed strongly with a Passmark database score of 26,527.8 and Redis SET operations reaching 72.52 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput was also top-tier at 86.13 GB/sec.

Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, the i7i.12xlarge is optimized for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and data-intensive applications. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in CPU-based machine learning tasks, with LLM inference text generation reaching 368.87 tokens/sec for small models and 31.03 tokens/sec for medium models. However, for massive 70B parameter models, text generation performance drops to an average of 3.31 tokens/sec, indicating a tradeoff when running very large models without dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8559C processor. It delivers 48 dedicated vCPUs, 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, and a bundled 11,250 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, the CPU features a 320 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and AMX instructions. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in Geekbench scores, static web serving at 86.13 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations at 72.52 million operations per second. The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 28 Gbps. With its high-capacity local storage and strong memory bandwidth, the i7i.12xlarge is highly efficient for database operations, high-throughput web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks, offering a balanced resource-to-storage profile without the need for external storage attachments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 11,250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database workloads.

tagline: A storage-optimized instance combining high-capacity local NVMe SSDs with DDR5 memory for demanding database and data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with 320 MB L3 cache
384 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
11,250 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in database operations and static web serving
Optimized for large-scale databases and high-throughput workloads
